title: Can't get error from 'setundef' command.
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

How to get this error from the command: "One of the options -zero, -one, -anyseq, -anyconst, or -random <seed> must be specified."

I have tried this command but didn't get an error:
`setundef -random 256 -zero -one -anyseq -anyconst
`

**But this error is reported when use this command:**
```
read_verilog top.v
setundef -undriven
```
20. Executing SETUNDEF pass (replace undef values with defined constants).
ERROR: One of the options -zero, -one, -anyseq, -anyconst, or -random <seed> must be specified.

**And should be:**

ERROR: The 'setundef' command can't operate in -undriven mode on modules with processes. Run 'proc' first.

**test case:**
```
module top
(
 input x,
 input y,
 input cin,

 output reg A,
 output cout
 );
 parameter X = 1;
 wire o;

`ifndef BUG
always @(posedge cin)
	A <= o;

//assign cout =  cin? y : x;

middle u_mid (.x(x),.o(o));
`else
assign {cout,A} =  cin - y * x;
`endif

endmodule

module middle
(
	input x,
	input y,
	output o
);

assign o = x + y;
endmodule
```
## Expected behavior

ERROR: One of the options -zero, -one, -anyseq, -anyconst, or -random <seed> must be specified.

## Actual behavior

No errors.
